claimStatusLegend.supported: Safe to describe as working now inside the current repo proof boundary.
claimStatusLegend.supported-local-only: Working for documented local lanes, but not a broad cross-surface guarantee.
claimStatusLegend.control-plane-only: Read/lifecycle/control behavior exists, but not the live runtime semantics implied by the larger capability name.
claimStatusLegend.bootstrap-only: Bootstrap or attach proof exists, but not live forwarding.
claimStatusLegend.connectable-preview: Users can reach a visible MCPace endpoint or contract, but runtime/session/host proof is still incomplete.
claimStatusLegend.requires-host-proof: Source-level behavior exists, but the support claim waits on real supported hosts.
claimStatusLegend.planned: Not yet strong enough to expose as working behavior.

features.title: Client catalog and adapter manifest loading
features.summary: client list loads verified and generic client targets from a Rust-side catalog instead of hard-coding one narrow set of brands in the schema.
features.nextStep: Keep the catalog current and move install/export logic into manifest-driven patchers instead of runtime brand branches.

features.title: Surface-aware client catalog
features.summary: The catalog distinguishes local, cloud, and API connector surfaces so compatibility is modeled by surface instead of brand alone.
features.nextStep: Keep adding documented surfaces and retire assumptions that one product name implies one transport/auth model.

features.title: Client, session, and project context resolution
features.summary: client plan resolves client identity, project roots, cwd, session signals, and richer metadata without starting a hub runtime.
features.nextStep: Reuse this logic from the future hub instead of duplicating it.

features.title: MCP roots and optional _meta context parsing
features.summary: The planner understands MCP-style roots objects, file:// URIs, and optional com.mcpace/context hints.
features.nextStep: Preserve the same contract in the future stdio shim and HTTP ingress.

features.title: Derived internal session leases
features.summary: When an external session id is missing, the planner derives a stable fallback lease and warns that the future hub must keep it sticky.
features.nextStep: Carry the derived lease model forward into the live hub and ingress layers.

features.title: Project root resolution and ambiguity handling
features.summary: The planner resolves project roots from flags, env, single-root metadata, or roots+cwd and warns when multiple roots remain ambiguous.
features.nextStep: Mirror these rules in the live hub and session registry.

features.title: Credential profile aware partitioning
features.summary: Credential-scoped servers now prefer an explicit credential profile id and only fall back to coarse binding classes when necessary.
features.nextStep: Feed this with real credential profile identities once the runtime owns auth flows.

features.title: Conservative scope and concurrency arbitration
features.summary: Unknown scope classes and unknown concurrency policies fall back to isolation or conservative serialization instead of optimistic sharing.
features.nextStep: Keep the same conservative defaults when the live hub starts reusing instances.

features.title: Surface constraint warnings during planning
features.summary: client plan warns for tools-only, public-http-only, no-remote-oauth, deprecated SSE, and tool-budget surfaces.
features.nextStep: Keep the warning layer honest and promote only those constraints that become runtime-enforced behavior.

features.title: Runtime scenario fixture set
features.summary: The repo carries production-like runtime fixtures separated into typical, edge, adversarial, compatibility, and held-out cases.
features.nextStep: Keep adding real regressions from future traces and failures.

features.title: Surface-aware coverage and gap reporting
features.summary: mcpace lab now reports coverage and gaps by client family, surface class, surface kind, and documented constraints.
features.nextStep: Use this surface breakdown to prioritize cloud/local divergence work instead of only counting scenarios.

features.title: Held-out release gate lane
features.summary: Held-out runtime fixtures exist so release gates can reserve scenarios that day-to-day iteration does not tune against.
features.nextStep: Keep at least one release-only scenario blocked from daily optimization.

features.title: Live hub daemon lifecycle
features.summary: The repo ships `init`, `hub up/down/status/logs/repair`, file-backed lease storage with an active-session registry, request-time lease gates with heartbeat renewal, settings-only conservative leases, lost-lease cancellation, stale-id filtering, and bounded in-process upstream session pooling for explicit upstream wrapper calls, but it still lacks durable cross-process pool ownership and transport-level cancellation.
features.nextStep: Harden the current in-process pool into durable stdio/HTTP session ownership with per-upstream concurrency/backpressure and transport-level cancellation on top of the current lifecycle, request lease gates, and active-session registry.

features.title: stdio shim that normalizes initialize metadata
features.summary: `mcpace stdio-shim --json` exists as a bootstrap-only proof surface that normalizes context, derives a sticky lease, reuses planner/export logic, and ensures the persistent hub is up, but it does not forward live MCP stdio traffic.
features.nextStep: Promote the existing bootstrap surface into live stdio forwarding without forking planning logic.

features.title: Local Streamable HTTP ingress with session handling
features.summary: `mcpace serve` and the local `/mcp` endpoint expose a connectable MCPace HTTP surface, and explicit upstream wrapper calls now acquire/release scheduler leases with heartbeat renewal, settings-only conservative leases, lost-heartbeat cancellation, and bounded in-process upstream session pooling, but full HTTP session lifecycle and real-host proof for a Streamable HTTP runtime are still incomplete.
features.nextStep: Turn the current connectable local `/mcp` surface into a session-aware ingress with real-host proof, durable ownership semantics, and transport-level cancellation/stale-result guards.

features.title: Exclusive lease enforcement for single-session servers
features.summary: File-backed scheduler leases now block conflicting planned routes, maintain an active-session registry for current lease owners, and explicit stdio/HTTP upstream wrapper calls acquire, heartbeat-renew, cancel on lost heartbeat, conservatively lease settings-only servers, reuse same-context in-process upstream sessions, and release request leases around real upstream MCP calls.
features.nextStep: Harden pooled-session ownership with cross-process registry semantics, per-upstream backpressure, and transport-level stale-result protection before claiming broad runtime ownership.

features.title: Cancellation and stale-result guards
features.summary: Request-time upstream wrapper calls now ignore stale JSON-RPC ids, abort before accepting a result when heartbeat renewal loses the lease, expose active lease sessions for restart/cancel bookkeeping, and drop pooled upstream sessions on protocol/timeout/lease-loss errors; transport-level cancellation and durable cross-process pooled-session registries are still missing.
features.nextStep: Track in-flight requests per durable pooled session/lease, propagate cancellation through live transports, and keep adversarial fixtures for dead pooled sessions.

features.title: Health supervision and restart policy
features.summary: Hub health/state files and readiness views exist, but there is no live instance supervisor with backoff, degraded/draining transitions, or restart policy.
features.nextStep: Add per-instance state, restart backoff, and drain/degraded transitions.

features.title: Client install and export flows
features.summary: Local `client install` writes MCPace-managed config for selected local surfaces with dry-run/diff previews and restoreable backups, and `client export` emits connectable MCPace contracts plus preview blockers for other lanes, but a broader manifest-driven config-writing export path is not complete yet.
features.nextStep: Split install proof from broader export work and extend config-writing export only after ingress/runtime proof stabilizes.

features.title: Safe config merge and owned-block patching
features.summary: Current local installers replace only the MCPace-managed block, stay idempotent in covered fixtures, support dry-run/diff previews, create restoreable backups, warn during Codex TOML install when preserved stdio entries point at missing commands, surface the same broken-entry class through verify readiness clientConfigWarnings, and have real-looking JSON/YAML merge regressions covering user-owned Claude settings, existing MCP servers, Hermes comments, later YAML sections, and restore round-trip.
features.nextStep: Keep adding real-client fixtures as new catalog patchers land, keep preserved-third-party-entry diagnostics non-destructive, and broaden config-writing export only after ingress/runtime proof stabilizes.

features.title: Real-client compatibility matrix
features.summary: The repo has a surface-aware catalog, but not yet a nightly lab against real Codex, Claude, Cursor, Kiro, Windsurf, Copilot, Gemini, or Hermes surfaces.
features.nextStep: Build a compatibility lane that captures initialize, session, roots, and auth traces from real hosts and turns them into held-out regressions.

features.title: Tools-only surface enforcement
features.summary: The surface catalog and `client plan` warn about tools-only, public-http-only, and no-remote-oauth limits, but install/export/runtime do not yet enforce those constraints end-to-end.
features.nextStep: Carry tools-only/public-http constraints from planning into install/export and runtime gates, then add real-host traces.

features.title: Tool-budget-aware export and enablement
features.summary: Tool-budget surfaces are cataloged and warned about, but MCPace does not yet budget or rank tool exposure automatically.
features.nextStep: Track per-surface tool budgets and build export heuristics that keep high-value tools within those limits.

features.title: Cloud or brokered relay path
features.summary: A purely local daemon cannot cover every cloud or API-driven MCP surface that expects a public HTTP endpoint.
features.nextStep: Design a separate relay or public HTTP lane after the local correctness slice is proven.
